gloss.en: the act of discovering or noticing something hidden

words.example: Early detection of the disease saves lives.

gloss.en: the state of being kept in a place and not allowed to leave

words.example: He spent two days in police detention.

gloss.en: to become worse

words.example: Her health deteriorated rapidly last winter.

gloss.en: to completely destroy a place or area

words.example: The flood devastated the small town.

gloss.en: the most powerful evil being

words.example: In the story, the devil tempts the young man.

gloss.en: to invent a new thing or new way of doing something

words.example: They devised a new way to save water.

gloss.en: to say exactly what an illness or problem is

words.example: The doctor diagnosed her illness quickly.

gloss.en: the act of identifying the exact cause of an illness or problem

words.example: The diagnosis was a serious heart problem.

gloss.en: to tell somebody what to do, often in an annoying way

words.example: You can't dictate how I live my life.

gloss.en: a leader with complete power over a country

words.example: The country was ruled by a cruel dictator.

gloss.en: to recognize or show that two things are not the same

words.example: It is hard to differentiate the twins.

gloss.en: a calm, serious manner that deserves respect

words.example: She faced the bad news with dignity.

gloss.en: a difficult choice between two equally important things

words.example: I faced a real dilemma about which job to take.

gloss.en: a measurement such as height, width or length

words.example: We measured the dimension of the room.

gloss.en: to become or make smaller or weaker

words.example: The world's resources are rapidly diminishing.

gloss.en: to put something quickly into a liquid and take it out

words.example: He dipped the brush into the paint.

gloss.en: a person who represents their country in a foreign country

words.example: The diplomat met leaders from both nations.

gloss.en: connected with relations between countries

words.example: The two nations cut diplomatic ties.

gloss.en: a list of names, addresses or phone numbers

words.example: I found his number in the phone directory.

gloss.en: very bad, harmful or unsuccessful

words.example: The trip ended in a disastrous storm.

gloss.en: to get rid of something you no longer want

words.example: Please discard the old newspapers.

gloss.en: to officially allow or make somebody leave

words.example: He was discharged from the army after his injury.

gloss.en: to give information about something secret

words.example: They refused to disclose the secret details.

gloss.en: the act of making secret information known

words.example: The disclosure of the documents shocked everyone.

gloss.en: a long, serious discussion of a subject

words.example: She wrote a long discourse on modern art.

gloss.en: the freedom to decide what should be done

words.example: I'll leave the choice to your discretion.

gloss.en: treating a group less fairly than others

words.example: The law bans racial discrimination at work.

gloss.en: the act of dismissing somebody from their job

words.example: He claimed his dismissal was unfair.

gloss.en: to take the place of somebody or something

words.example: Machines have displaced many factory workers.

gloss.en: the act of getting rid of something

words.example: The city handles waste disposal each week.

gloss.en: to get rid of, or arrange things in a way

words.example: Please dispose of the trash properly.

gloss.en: an argument between people, groups or countries

words.example: The two firms had a long pay dispute.

gloss.en: to make it hard for something to continue normally

words.example: The storm disrupted the train service.

gloss.en: a situation where something cannot continue normally

words.example: The strike caused major disruption to traffic.

gloss.en: to mix with a liquid and become part of it

words.example: Salt dissolves quickly in warm water.

gloss.en: a clear difference between similar things

words.example: There is a clear distinction between the two plans.

gloss.en: having a quality that makes something easily noticed

words.example: She wore a dress with a distinctive style.

gloss.en: to change shape or sound so it is strange or unclear

words.example: The mirror can distort your shape.

gloss.en: a feeling of great worry or unhappiness

words.example: The sad news caused her great distress.

gloss.en: making you feel anxious, upset or shocked

words.example: We heard some disturbing news today.

gloss.en: to make somebody or something change direction

words.example: Police diverted the traffic around the accident.

gloss.en: coming from or connected with God

words.example: They believed the king had divine power.

gloss.en: a belief taught by a church, party, etc.

words.example: The party follows a strict economic doctrine.

gloss.en: documents that give proof or evidence of something

words.example: I didn't have the necessary documentation to enter.

gloss.en: an area of knowledge or activity

words.example: Financial matters are her domain.

gloss.en: the fact of being more powerful or important

words.example: The team kept its dominance over rivals.

gloss.en: a person or group that gives money or goods

words.example: A generous donor gave money to the school.

gloss.en: an amount of medicine taken at one time

words.example: Take one dose of this medicine each night.

gloss.en: to make something empty or dry by removing liquid

words.example: Drain and rinse the pasta before serving.

gloss.en: to move slowly and smoothly in water or air

words.example: Clouds drifted across the blue sky.

gloss.en: strong and having a powerful influence

words.example: She was the driving force behind the project.

gloss.en: to die from being underwater and unable to breathe

words.example: Two children drowned in the river.

gloss.en: having two parts or aspects

words.example: He has a dual role as writer and editor.

gloss.en: to give somebody or something a particular name

words.example: The press dubbed her the queen of pop.

gloss.en: stupid

words.example: That was a pretty dumb thing to do.

gloss.en: two people who perform or are seen together

words.example: The comedy duo made the crowd laugh.

gloss.en: full of energy and having a strong personality

words.example: They need a dynamic leader for the team.

gloss.en: very interested and excited about something

words.example: The children were eager to open their gifts.

gloss.en: the money you earn for the work you do

words.example: His earnings rose after the promotion.

gloss.en: lack of difficulty

words.example: He passed the exam with ease.

gloss.en: a sound that is reflected back off a surface

words.example: We heard an echo in the empty cave.

gloss.en: connected with living things and their environment

words.example: We must protect the ecological balance.

gloss.en: a person whose job is to teach people

words.example: She is a respected educator in her field.

gloss.en: the fact of producing the result that is wanted

words.example: We tested the effectiveness of the new drug.

gloss.en: doing something well with no waste of time or money

words.example: The new machine improved factory efficiency.

gloss.en: your sense of your own value and importance

words.example: He has a very big ego.

gloss.en: very complicated and full of detail

words.example: They made elaborate plans for the wedding.

gloss.en: connected with elections

words.example: The country needs electoral reform.

gloss.en: to give somebody a higher position or rank

words.example: The win elevated her to a national hero.

gloss.en: able to have or do something because you qualify

words.example: Only people over 70 are eligible for the payment.

gloss.en: a small group of powerful or rich people

words.example: He was a member of the ruling elite.

gloss.en: to get onto a ship or plane; to start something

words.example: They embarked on a long sea journey.

gloss.en: shy or uncomfortable feelings

words.example: I nearly died of embarrassment.

gloss.en: officials who represent their government abroad

words.example: She works at the French embassy in Tokyo.

gloss.en: to fix something firmly in a substance or object

words.example: A piece of glass was embedded in his leg.

gloss.en: to express or represent an idea or quality

words.example: She embodies the spirit of the team.

gloss.en: the fact of appearing or becoming known

words.example: The emergence of new tech changed the market.

gloss.en: based on experiments or experience, not theory

words.example: They need empirical evidence to prove it.

gloss.en: to give somebody the power or authority to act

words.example: The law empowers police to search homes.

gloss.en: to pass a law

words.example: Parliament enacted a new tax law.

gloss.en: to include a large number or range of things

words.example: The job encompasses many responsibilities.

gloss.en: the act of giving somebody support or hope

words.example: A few words of encouragement helped a lot.

gloss.en: giving somebody support, courage or hope

words.example: The test results were very encouraging.

gloss.en: an attempt to do something new or difficult

words.example: Their endeavour to climb the peak failed.

gloss.en: very large in amount and seeming to have no end

words.example: She showed endless patience with the kids.

gloss.en: to say publicly that you support somebody or something

words.example: I fully endorse his idea.

gloss.en: a public statement of support for somebody or something

words.example: The win was a clear endorsement of his policies.

gloss.en: to deal with something painful without giving up

words.example: They had to endure a long, hard winter.

gloss.en: to make sure people obey a law or rule

words.example: It is the police's job to enforce the law.

gloss.en: the act of making people obey a law or rule

words.example: Strict enforcement of the rules is needed.

gloss.en: an agreement to marry; the period of being engaged

words.example: Their engagement was announced last week.

gloss.en: interesting or pleasant in an attractive way

words.example: She has an engaging smile.

gloss.en: to ask somebody for some information

words.example: I called to enquire about train times.

gloss.en: to improve the quality of something

words.example: Reading can enrich your mind.

gloss.en: to officially join a course, school, etc.

words.example: You need to enrol before August ends.

gloss.en: to happen after or as a result of an event

words.example: A heated argument ensued.

gloss.en: a company or business

words.example: He runs a small family enterprise.

gloss.en: a person who is very interested in something

words.example: He is a football enthusiast.

gloss.en: to give somebody the right to have or do something

words.example: This ticket entitles you to a free drink.

gloss.en: something that exists separately with its own identity

words.example: The two firms became one legal entity.

gloss.en: many cases of a disease at the same time

words.example: The town faced a flu epidemic.

gloss.en: being equal in rights, status or advantages

words.example: They fought for gender equality.

gloss.en: a statement showing two values are equal

words.example: Solve the equation for x.

gloss.en: to build something

words.example: They erected a statue in the square.

gloss.en: to become or make greater, worse or more serious

words.example: The small fight quickly escalated.

gloss.en: the most important quality or feature of something

words.example: His paintings capture the essence of spring.

gloss.en: an organization, large institution or hotel

words.example: She studies at a well-known educational establishment.

gloss.en: without an end; lasting forever

words.example: They promised eternal love.

gloss.en: to move people from danger to a safer place

words.example: Police evacuated the nearby buildings.

gloss.en: to bring a feeling, memory or image to mind

words.example: The song evoked memories of her youth.

gloss.en: connected with slow, steady development and change

words.example: Scientists study evolutionary theory.

gloss.en: to make something seem larger or worse than it is

words.example: Don't exaggerate how bad it was.

gloss.en: the quality of being extremely good

words.example: The school has a reputation for excellence.

gloss.en: unusually good

words.example: She has an exceptional talent for music.

gloss.en: more than what is necessary or usual

words.example: Excess food is stored as fat.

gloss.en: the act of keeping somebody or something out

words.example: He was upset about his exclusion from the team.

gloss.en: used by only one person or group

words.example: The club is exclusive to members.

gloss.en: for only one person, group or use

words.example: This room is used exclusively for staff.

gloss.en: to kill somebody as a legal punishment

words.example: He was executed for treason.

gloss.en: the act of killing somebody as a legal punishment

words.example: He faced execution for the crime.
